lemma should capture the essence of why applying map fst to the list returned by alist_of (Abs_pq (...)) is the same as <br/>directly applying map fst to the original list insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q).<br/>### new lemma2<br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New p.font:
To properly justify the step "map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)))) = map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))" in
the proof of the lemma push_values1, we need to introduce and prove an intermediate lemma that formalizes this reasoning step.
This intermediate lemma should capture the essence of why applying map fst to the list returned by alist_of (Abs_pq (...)) is the same as
directly applying map fst to the original list insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q).
### new lemma2

p.font:
: the (map_of (alist_of q) (fst (hd (alist_of q)))) \<le> the (map_of (alist_of q) v) In Isabelle, this step cannot be verified by
sledgehammer because the reasoning rules are either unclear or the reasoning gap is too large.
